title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers express middleware, promise errors, dependencies, and response ownership into a new composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Middleware Order Short Circuit
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 3186 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Middleware Order Short Circuit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Middleware Order Short Circuit, keep Middleware Order Short Circuit connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Transport Domain Response Owner, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 8325 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Transport Domain Response Owner, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A layered service requires many pass-through classes and lets any layer send the response.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Transport Domain Response Owner happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Transport Domain Response Owner condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Transport Domain Response Owner
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Transport Domain Response Owner: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 6706 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Transport Domain Response Owner.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Transport Domain Response Owner, keep Transport Domain Response Owner connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.
